ListItem.Para: Center tank quantity indication operates normally.
Appropriate amount of center tank fuel is considered unusable and is accounted for in the airplane weight and balance.

Note.Para: AFM fuel loading and usage limitations are for usable fuel.
For one scavenge pump inoperative, fuel imbalance will occur when center tank fuel is scavenged.

ListItem.Para: For center tank fuel required:
Increase flight planning fuel by 4800 lb (2177 kg).
Reduce the maximum zero fuel weight limit by 4800 lb (2177 kg).
Adjust the zero fuel weight center of gravity forward limit 0.5% MAC aft.
For center tank fuel not required:
Center tank fuel at or above 4800 lb (2177 kg):
Reduce the maximum zero fuel weight limit by 4800 lb (2177 kg).
Adjust the zero fuel weight center of gravity forward limit 0.5% MAC aft.
Center tank fuel less than 4800 lb (2177 kg):
Reduce the maximum zero fuel weight limit by the amount of center tank fuel.
Adjust the zero fuel weight center of gravity forward limit based on the amount of center tank fuel.
For dispatches with center tank fuel quantities between 4800 lb (2177 kg) and 16,000 lb (7257 kg) (FUEL IN CENTER advisory message not display):
After cruise altitude has been established, set both center tank pump switches on.

Note.Para: Fuel usage from the center tank may be delayed up to 30 minutes.

ListItem.Para: When FUEL LOW CENTER advisory message is displayed, set both center tank pump switches off.
Inoperative valve is locked closed.
Center tank quantity indication operates normally.
Appropriate amount of center tank fuel is considered unusable and is accounted for in the airplane weight and balance.

Note.Para: AFM fuel loading and usage limitations are for usable fuel.

MaintenanceProcedure.Para: Lock the inoperative scavenge isolation valve closed (AMM DDG 28-22-04).

ListItem.Para: For left scavenge isolation valve inoperative, using a flight deck multi-function display only, open the following circuit breakers and lock with an INOP tag:
SCAVENGE ISLN VLV CLOSE-L
SCAVENGE ISLN VLV OPEN-L
For right scavenge valve inoperative, using a flight deck multi-function display only, open the following circuit breakers and lock with an INOP tag:
SCAVENGE ISLN VLV CLOSE-R
SCAVENGE ISLN VLV OPEN-R
Open and deactivate the landing gear doors.
Gain access to the inoperative scavenge isolation valve in the main gear wheel well.
Disconnect, cap and stow the valve electrical connector.
Lock the valve closed.
Close the landing gear doors.

Note.Para: For one scavenge valve inoperative, fuel imbalance will occur when center tank fuel is scavenged.
The fuel synoptic will incorrectly display a green flow line for the deactivated scavenge valve when the center tank is or would normally be scavenged.

ListItem.Para: For center tank fuel required:
Increase flight planning fuel by 4800 lb (2177 kg).
Reduce the maximum zero fuel weight limit by 4800 lb (2177 kg).
Adjust the zero fuel weight center of gravity forward limit 0.5% MAC aft.
For center tank fuel not required:
Center tank fuel at or above 4800 lb (2177 kg):
Reduce the maximum zero fuel weight limit by 4800 lb (2177 kg).
Adjust the zero fuel weight center of gravity forward limit 0.5% MAC aft.
Center tank fuel less than 4800 lb (2177 kg):
Reduce the maximum zero fuel weight limit by the amount of center tank fuel.
Adjust the zero fuel weight center of gravity forward limit based on the amount of center tank fuel.
For dispatches with center tank fuel quantities between 4800 lb (2177 kg) and 16,000 lb (7257 kg) (FUEL IN CENTER advisory message not display):
After cruise altitude has been established, set both center tank pump switches on.

Note.Para: Fuel usage from the center tank may be delayed up to 30 minutes.

ListItem.Para: When FUEL LOW CENTER advisory message is displayed, set both center tank pump switches off.
